questions.stem: An 8-year-old boy is brought to your office by his anxious mother who says that he cannot read at grade level. She says he was doing well until last year in second grade when he began falling behind. He has become somewhat of a behavioral problem in school since his troubles with reading began. His mother insists that he is “at least as smart as every other kid in that school” but says she would be relieved to hear that he has a learning disorder “so he can get the help he needs.” You read with the child and confirm that he is having difficulty. You should do which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tell the mother to schedule some sessions with a reading specialist

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ask the child if he likes to read

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Inattentive 'not listening' child — exclude sensory deficit first → test the child's vision/hearing.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Reassure the mother that her son is doing fine

choices.label: H
choices.explanation: Conduct disorder.

choices.explanation: Inattentive 'not listening' child — exclude sensory deficit first → test the child's vision/hearing.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Reassure the mother that her son is doing fine

choices.label: H

questions.stem: Which of the following is a relative contraindication to ECT? (A) a space-occupying lesion of the brain (B) a history of seizures (C) diabetes mellitus (D) a small-bowel obstruction (E) catatonia

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Main ECT contraindication (raised ICP) → space-occupying brain lesion.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Agent to continue around ECT → haloperidol.

flag.correctAnswer: a space-occupying lesion of the brain (not among the listed options)
flag.source: RCPsych ECT guidance; Maudsley Prescribing Guidelines
flag.note: A space-occupying lesion with raised intracranial pressure is the classic contraindication to ECT. The correct answer is NOT among the listed (drug) options, and the keyed option (lithium) does not answer the question. Treat this item's option set as flawed.
